chapters.title: The Toys Need Help
chapters.text:
A long train was carrying toys and good food to children on the other side of the mountain. The train had toy elephants, giraffes, and dolls. It also had milk and apples.

All the toys and food were for good little boys and girls. But then something bad happened. The engine pulling the train broke down. It could not move another inch.

"What will we do?" asked the toy clown. "The children are waiting for us!" said the toy elephant. "We need help," said all the toys together. "We must get over the mountain!"

The toys looked down the track. They saw another engine coming their way. It was a big, strong passenger engine. Maybe this engine would help them get over the mountain.

vocabulary.definition: taking something from one place to another

vocabulary.sentence: "broke down" is used in this chapter.

informationalText.title: Butterflies
informationalText.content:
Butterflies start their lives as tiny eggs laid on leaves. When the eggs hatch, little caterpillars come out and start eating the leaves. The caterpillars eat and eat until they grow bigger and bigger. After a few weeks, each caterpillar makes a hard shell called a chrysalis around itself.
Inside the chrysalis, something amazing happens! The caterpillar changes into a beautiful butterfly with colorful wings. This change is called metamorphosis. When the butterfly is ready, it breaks out of the chrysalis and flies away. Adult butterflies drink sweet nectar from flowers and help plants by carrying pollen from flower to flower.

questions.question: What do caterpillars do after they hatch from eggs?
What is the change from caterpillar to butterfly called?

journalPrompt.prompt: How would you feel if you were in this story? Write about your thoughts.

chapters.title: Big Engines Say No
chapters.text:
The toys called out to the big passenger engine. "Please help us! We need to get over the mountain to the children!" But the big engine shook his head.

"I do not pull toy trains," said the passenger engine. "I only pull important people in fancy cars. I am too important for your job." And away he went.

Soon another engine came down the track. This one was a big freight engine. He was strong and could pull heavy things. Surely he would help the toys!

"Please help us!" called the toys. But the freight engine said, "I am too tired. I have been working all day pulling heavy cars. Find someone else to help you."

vocabulary.definition: goods or cargo carried by train

comprehension.question: Why wouldn't the passenger engine help?
options: He thought he was too important

comprehension.question: What excuse did the freight engine give?

informationalText.title: Dentists
informationalText.content:
Dentists are doctors who take care of people's teeth and gums. They help keep our mouths healthy by cleaning our teeth and checking for problems. Dentists use special tools to look inside our mouths and take X-ray pictures of our teeth. They can see cavities, which are little holes in teeth caused by bacteria.
When dentists find cavities, they can fix them by filling the holes with special materials. Dentists also teach people how to brush and floss their teeth properly to prevent cavities. Most people should visit the dentist twice a year for a checkup and cleaning. Going to the dentist regularly helps keep our teeth strong and healthy for our whole lives.

questions.question: What are cavities?
options: little holes in teeth caused by bacteria

questions.question: How often should people visit the dentist?

chapters.title: Little Engine Volunteers
chapters.text:
The toys were feeling very sad. Two big, strong engines had said no. How would they ever get to the children on the other side of the mountain?

Then they heard a little voice. "Maybe I can help you," said a small blue engine. She was much smaller than the other engines. She had never pulled a train over a mountain before.

"You are very small," said the toy clown. "Do you think you are strong enough?" The Little Blue Engine looked at the mountain. It was very high and steep.

"I have never done it before," said the Little Blue Engine. "But I will try! The children need their toys and food. I think I can do it!"

vocabulary.definition: offers to help without being asked
having a sharp slope; very hard to climb

comprehension.question: What color was the little engine?
Had the Little Blue Engine ever pulled a train over a mountain before?

informationalText.title: Rain
informationalText.content:
Rain is water that falls from clouds in the sky. It starts when the sun heats up water in oceans, rivers, and lakes, turning it into invisible water vapor that rises into the air. High up in the sky, the water vapor cools down and forms tiny water droplets that stick to dust particles in the air.
These tiny droplets join together to make bigger droplets inside clouds. When the droplets get heavy enough, they fall down as rain. Rain is very important because it gives plants the water they need to grow. It also fills up rivers, lakes, and underground water sources that people and animals need for drinking.

questions.question: How does rain start?
options: the sun heats up water and turns it into water vapor
the wind pushes water from the ocean

questions.question: Why is rain important?
options: it gives plants water and fills up rivers and lakes

chapters.title: Going Up the Mountain
chapters.text:
The Little Blue Engine hooked herself to the train. She took a deep breath. The mountain looked very tall. But she was determined to help the toys get to the children.

She started to pull. The train was heavy with all the toys and food. At first, it was hard to get moving. But slowly, the Little Blue Engine began to chug up the track.

The mountain got steeper and steeper. The Little Blue Engine huffed and puffed. She was working harder than she had ever worked before. But she did not give up.

The toys watched as the Little Blue Engine struggled up the mountain. They cheered for her. "You can do it!" they called. "We believe in you!" The engine smiled and kept going.

vocabulary.definition: having made up your mind to do something
tried very hard to do something difficult

comprehension.question: What made the train hard to pull?
options: It was heavy with toys and food

comprehension.question: How did the toys help the Little Blue Engine?

informationalText.title: Police Officers
informationalText.content:
Police officers are people who work to keep communities safe. They patrol neighborhoods in police cars, on foot, or on bicycles to watch for problems and help people who need assistance. Police officers learn about laws and how to help solve conflicts between people. They also direct traffic and make sure drivers follow traffic rules.
When someone breaks the law or when there is an emergency, people call the police for help. Police officers respond quickly to help solve problems and keep everyone safe. They also visit schools and community centers to teach children about safety and being good citizens. Many police officers work with special partners like police dogs who help them do their jobs.

questions.question: What is the main job of police officers?
Where do police officers patrol?
options: neighborhoods in cars, on foot, or on bicycles

only on highways

chapters.title: I Think I Can!
chapters.text:
The mountain was getting steeper. The Little Blue Engine was getting tired. But she kept saying to herself, "I think I can! I think I can! I think I can!"

With each chug up the mountain, she said it again. "I think I can! I think I can!" The words gave her strength. She kept pulling the heavy train.

The toys began to say it with her. "I think I can! I think I can!" they all chanted together. Their voices echoed off the mountain walls.

Higher and higher they climbed. The Little Blue Engine was working so hard. But she never stopped believing in herself. "I think I can! I think I can! I think I can!"

vocabulary.definition: the power to do hard things
bounced back as a repeated sound

comprehension.question: What did the Little Blue Engine keep saying to herself?
Who started saying the words with the Little Blue Engine?

chapters.title: Success and Celebration
chapters.text:
Finally, after much hard work, the Little Blue Engine reached the top of the mountain! She had done it! She had pulled the train all the way up the steep mountain.

Now going down the other side was easy. As they rolled down toward the town, the Little Blue Engine said happily, "I thought I could! I thought I could! I thought I could!"

The toys cheered loudly. "Hooray for the Little Blue Engine!" they shouted. "She saved the day! The children will get their toys and food because of her!"

The Little Blue Engine felt very proud. She had learned that being small did not matter. What mattered was trying your best and never giving up. She knew she could do anything!

vocabulary.definition: doing what you set out to do
feeling good about what you have done

comprehension.question: What did the Little Blue Engine say as she went down the mountain?
What lesson did the Little Blue Engine learn?
options: Being small doesn't matter if you try your best

Mountains are easy to climb
